name: slack
description: Send messages, read channels, and receive real-time events from Slack
long_description:
Full Slack integration for Chitty Workspace. Send messages to channels, read conversation history, reply to threads, and receive real-time @mentions and DMs via Socket Mode.

**Messaging** — Send messages to any channel the bot is invited to. Reply in threads. Format with Slack mrkdwn.

**Reading** — List channels, read message history, search conversations. Summarize long threads.

**Real-Time Events** — Enable Socket Mode for persistent WebSocket connection. Receive @mentions and DMs, route to an agent for automatic responses.

Requires a Slack Developer App (free). Setup wizard guides you through app creation.

auth.instructions: This package uses OAuth to connect to your Slack workspace. You'll create a Slack App and authorize it.

setup_steps.label: Install Python dependencies
setup_steps.check_command: pip show slack_sdk && pip show chitty_sdk
setup_steps.install_command: pip install slack_sdk aiohttp keyring chitty-sdk
setup_steps.help_text: Python libraries needed for Slack API, secure credential storage, and Chitty SDK helpers.

setup_steps.label: Create Slack App
setup_steps.help_text:
Go to api.slack.com/apps and create a new app:
1. Click 'Create New App' → 'From scratch'
2. Name: Chitty Workspace, select your workspace
3. OAuth & Permissions → Redirect URLs → Add: http://localhost:8770/oauth/callback
4. Bot Token Scopes → Add all required scopes
5. Socket Mode → Enable → Generate App-Level Token (connections:write)
6. Event Subscriptions → Enable → Subscribe: app_mention, message.im
7. Install App to workspace

setup_steps.label: Enter Slack Client ID
setup_steps.prompt_placeholder: Found in Basic Information → App Credentials
setup_steps.prompt_help: Copy the Client ID from your Slack App's Basic Information page.

setup_steps.label: Enter Slack Client Secret
setup_steps.prompt_placeholder: Found in Basic Information → App Credentials
setup_steps.prompt_help: Copy the Client Secret from your Slack App's Basic Information page.

setup_steps.label: Connect to Slack workspace
setup_steps.help_text: Click 'Connect' to authorize the app in your Slack workspace. A browser window will open for authorization.

setup_steps.label: Enter App-Level Token (Socket Mode)
setup_steps.prompt_help: From Basic Information → App-Level Tokens. Generate one with 'connections:write' scope. Starts with 'xapp-'.

configurable_resources.label: Allowed Channels
configurable_resources.description: Select which channels the agent can read and send messages to. Accepts both #channel-name and channel IDs (e.g. C01ABC23DEF). Leave empty to allow all channels the bot is invited to.

feature_flags.label: Allow sending messages
feature_flags.description: When enabled, the agent can post messages and reply to threads in Slack channels.
feature_flags.enable_warning: The agent will be able to post messages as your Slack bot.

feature_flags.label: Allow reading channel history
feature_flags.description: When enabled, the agent can read message history from Slack channels. Disable to restrict the agent to sending only.
feature_flags.enable_warning: The agent will be able to read message history from allowed channels.

feature_flags.label: Enable real-time events (Socket Mode)
feature_flags.description: When enabled, maintains a persistent WebSocket connection to receive @mentions and DMs.
feature_flags.enable_warning: Enables a persistent background connection to Slack. Requires an App-Level Token.

connections.label: Real-time Events (Socket Mode)
connections.description: Persistent WebSocket connection to receive @mentions, DMs, and slash commands from Slack in real-time.

events.label: @Mention
events.description: Triggered when someone @mentions the bot in a channel

events.label: Direct Message
events.description: Triggered when someone sends a direct message to the bot

events.label: Slash Command
events.description: Triggered when someone uses the /datavisions slash command

agent_config.default_instructions: You have access to Slack tools. Always list channels first to confirm access. Show message content to the user before sending. Never post without explicit confirmation. Respect channel allowlists and feature flags -- do not attempt to bypass them.
suggested_prompts: Show me the last 10 messages in #general
Send a message to #engineering: Weekly update is ready
capabilities: Read message history from channels (when allow_read_history is enabled)
Send messages to channels (when allow_send_message is enabled)
